trip.name: Toronto → Texas → West Coast → Rockies → Home (36 days)
statsChips: One leg at a time — you’ve got this
trip.overviewLead: 36-day Canada ↔ USA motorcycle loop in three chapters: east → Texas home → west coast, Cascades, Yellowstone, and home through the plains. After Luray: Roanoke, a full Blue Ridge Parkway day to Bristol, Smokies plus Tail of the Dragon with a Kingston night, then Parsons before Arkansas.
planningChecklist: Confirm dates, host addresses, and camping fees before you roll.
Days marked TBD still need pins — use each day’s Google Maps link to lock distance.
Fill fuel / weather / key notes per day as you research (same idea as your Word plan).
Summer wildfire smoke can hit NorCal, PNW, Rockies, and high plains on short notice — check AQI/smoke maps for your corridor the morning you ride; shorten or reroute when air is unhealthy.
Every iOverlander / BLM / dispersed GPS pin: confirm land status, MVUM, and fire closures the same week you stay — crowdsourced pins are not permission to camp.
trip.summary: 36-day ride stringing together the Finger Lakes, a New York metro stop, Washington DC and Luray, then Roanoke, Blue Ridge Parkway to Bristol, Great Smoky approaches with Tail of the Dragon and a Kingston night, Parsons TN, then Nashville–Memphis–Arkansas, a Fort Worth home stop, then the high plains and I-40 toward New Mexico. From there: San Juan Mountains and Million Dollar Highway, Utah (Mexican Hat, Page, Bryce, Moab with a full rest day at Arches, St. George), the California coast (Leo Carrillo day stop, Big Sur, redwoods), Oregon and the Columbia River Gorge, Seattle, US-20 and Spokane, Lolo Pass, Yellowstone and the Beartooth corridor, and a long return through the Dakotas and Great Lakes to Ontario, with a final night in London before Toronto.

days.title: Toronto → Horseheads, NY (Southern Tier / Finger Lakes)
days.routeLine: Plan in your mapping app — typical: GTA → Niagara/Lewiston or Peace Bridge corridor → I-90 east into NY → Finger Lakes roads (verify crossing and tolls).
days.fuelNotes: Fill in Ontario before the border if convenient; I-90 NY has frequent fuel after entry.
days.foodNotes: Pack snacks for border queue; rural Finger Lakes — know dinner plan with hosts or town stop.
days.weatherNotes: Lake breeze and frontal weather near Ontario / Erie — check wind on bridge approaches.
keyNotes: Border: registration, insurance, declare tools/spares as needed; keep answers simple (touring).
Save offline maps for NY Thruway and last-mile to Horseheads (Chemung County).
days.campingAccommodation: Host contact (call, text, email, drafts) is on this day below the stay summary; keep a state-park backup in mind if plans slip.
highlights: Evening in Horseheads / Southern Tier (Finger Lakes approaches)

lodging.heading: Stay (Bunk a Biker)
lodging.name: Jimmy & Kaylee Comfort
lodging.notes: Text fastest; yard camping or garage for bikes—confirm ETA and house rules (no drink-ride; 420 outside only). Full host profile on the contact card below.

feesAndPasses: No U.S. national park entrance on this day.
Canada: no outbound park fee. U.S. entry: CBP inspection has no park-style fee.
NY Thruway (I-90) / crossings: tolls apply — E-ZPass or Tolls By Mail (plate billing); Peace Bridge / Lewiston–Queenston bridge tolls vary — set up payment before trip if needed.

days.title: Finger Lakes → New York
days.weatherNotes: Approaching NYC / Long Island in summer: heat + humidity in full gear — plan water, shade, and rest; don’t rely on “not thirsty yet.”
highlights: Shorter leg before DC → Luray segment

lodging.heading: Stay options
lodging.name: Dan
lodging.notes: Long Island — confirm full street address and bike parking.

lodgingAlternatives.heading: Paid camping (NYS — often cheaper than Hamptons hotels)
lodgingAlternatives.name: Hither Hills State Park
lodgingAlternatives.notes: ~190 oceanfront campsites (West / East / J loops); hot showers, restrooms, dump station, camp store. **No pets** anywhere in the park. Permit holder **21+** must stay on site. **Montauk is ~55–70 mi east of Southampton** — only practical if you camp here instead of the LI host night, or you accept a long late-day ride. Book on **ReserveAmerica** (NYS contract) or **1-800-456-2267**; **same-day campsite booking until 3 p.m.** Walk-ins when sites exist. **Economy:** non–New York State residents pay **out-of-state campsite rates** (~2× resident posted price — check parks.ny.gov camping fees before you book). Add **~US$7.25 reservation fee** online; **second vehicle ~US$20** in overflow (one vehicle on pad). **Vehicle day-use fee** often applies in summer beach season (~**US$10**/vehicle during posted hours — verify on parks.ny.gov for your dates). Peak mid-summer: some loops **Sunday–Sunday 7-night** minimums; **J Loop** tent-only, **no generators**, **US$5 cash key-card deposit**. Off-peak late May: confirm **minimum-night** rules (non-peak can require **2 nights** online — call park or try walk-in for 1 night).

lodgingAlternatives.heading: Stay option
lodgingAlternatives.name: Courtney Schrader
lodgingAlternatives.notes: DC metro — useful if you route toward Luray via I-95.

risks: Metro traffic near NYC / Long Island
If camping Montauk same night as a west-LI host: unrealistic mileage — pick one region for the night.
days.planB: If the Long Island host night falls through: try **Hither Hills** (reserve/walk-in) or a motel farther west on LI — budget time from Montauk.
feesAndPasses: No federal park entrance fee on routing described.
NYC / Hudson crossings (if used): many bridges/tunnels are cashless tolling to a registered plate or bill-by-mail — budget tolls; motorcycles follow posted rules per crossing.

**Hither Hills State Park (optional paid camp, Montauk):** NYS facility — official hub **parks.ny.gov/parks/hither-hills** and camping search **newyorkstateparks.reserveamerica.com** (park id on ReserveAmerica: **Hither Hills / NY**). **Non-resident** campers pay higher posted rates than NY residents (historically on the order of **~US$66 weekday / ~US$74 weekend** vs **~US$33 / ~US$37** resident for standard tent/RV sites — **verify current table** on **parks.ny.gov/camping/fees-rates** before you pay).

Hither Hills extras to budget: **~US$7.25** online reservation fee per booking; **~US$20** second registered vehicle; possible **~US$10 vehicle use fee** during summer beach gate hours (dates/hours on park page); **J Loop** key card **US$5 cash** deposit (lost card fee).

Hither Hills rules that hit riders: **no pets**; **21+** registered camper must be on site; **generators** restricted (forbidden in J Loop); **check-in 3 p.m. / out 11 a.m.** typical — confirm on your reservation. **Motorcycles:** count toward vehicle limits — confirm with park office if one tent pad + bike.

days.title: New York → Washington, DC → Luray (VA)
days.weatherNotes: Mid-Atlantic late May: humid air and slow traffic can overheat you in protective gear — hydrate on a schedule through the I-95 / Beltway grind.

lodging.heading: Stay (Bunk a Biker)
lodging.name: Frank Allen Pence
lodging.notes: Text preferred — mention Bunk a Biker; host sends full street address. Full contact card below.

feesAndPasses: District of Columbia: no NPS entrance fee to ride public roads past monuments (no timed NPS entry ticket required just to transit).
If you enter a specific fee-charging NPS unit as a visitor attraction, pay that site’s fee — not required for through-riding to Luray.
Virginia: no Shenandoah NP fee unless you enter the Skyline Drive corridor (fee applies at entrance stations). Your Luray routing may stay outside park gates — confirm map.

days.title: Luray → Roanoke (VA)
days.routeLine: Luray area → Roanoke metro via US-11 / US-460 or I-81 options — pick traffic vs scenery in Maps; resupply in Roanoke, then run last miles to Hardy (BRP area ~7 mi from MP 112 per host).
days.fuelNotes: Fuel in Luray or before Roanoke; Roanoke has full services.
days.foodNotes: Stock water/snacks before leaving Roanoke if arriving late to the host.
days.weatherNotes: Late May: pop-up thunderstorms — check radar; avoid ridges in lightning.
keyNotes: Confirm ETA and bike parking with host (call/text).
If you bail to NF dispersed instead: Jefferson NF MVUM only — verify motor-vehicle access same week; pack out all waste.
days.campingAccommodation: Primary: Bunk a Biker stay at Hardy — host contact card below. Backup: see alternates for NF dispersed if plans change.
days.terrain: Shenandoah approaches → Roanoke valley → short run to Hardy

lodging.heading: Stay (Bunk a Biker)
lodging.name: Vicky Cawley
lodging.notes: Call or text to confirm arrival; garage, tools, lift, tent or indoor options per host profile.

lodgingAlternatives.heading: Backup (dispersed NF)
lodgingAlternatives.name: George Washington & Jefferson NF — near Roanoke (search MVUM)
lodgingAlternatives.notes: Use official Motor Vehicle Use Map for legal spur roads; verify same week. No amenities; 14-day limits common.

risks: Gravel/mud on NF roads after rain
days.planB: Developed NF campground (fee), other B.a.B. options, or motel in Roanoke/Salem if weather or fatigue wins.
feesAndPasses: Private host stay: no public-land fee — separate from NF rules below.

George Washington & Jefferson National Forests (if you use dispersed backup): no NPS entrance fee; dispersed camping is generally free where MVUM allows — developed NF campgrounds charge site fees if you use them.

days.title: Roanoke → Bristol (VA/TN) — Blue Ridge Parkway day
days.routeLine: Primary riding on Blue Ridge Parkway and short connectors — NOT I-81 as your main line. In Google Maps: set waypoints Roanoke → Rocky Knob / Mabry Mill (BRP) → Fancy Gap → Boone NC → secondary roads into Bristol TN/VA. Drag route off interstates; use ‘avoid highways’ if helpful. BRP max ~45 mph — full riding day.
days.fuelNotes: BRP has limited fuel — tank in Roanoke; next reliable fuel often Boone or after BRP exit toward Bristol.
days.foodNotes: Mabry Mill / visitor areas are limited — carry snacks.
days.weatherNotes: Elevation changes quickly — carry rain layer; fog on BRP mornings.
keyNotes: BRP is NPS: federal speed limits, wildlife (bear/deer), no commercial trucks.
Bristol sits on the TN/VA line — clocks and tax rules differ by side.
days.campingAccommodation: Free dispersed — Cherokee NF or Holston / TVA shoreline (verify)

stops.label: BRP landmark (optional)
stops.notes: Historic mill — short stop; parking fills weekends.

lodging.heading: Free camping (dispersed)
lodging.name: Cherokee NF / Holston Mountain area — verify MVUM
lodging.notes: Eastern TN / southwest VA has NF dispersed sites — confirm motor-vehicle access on your exact spur. Example search: Cherokee National Forest dispersed near Damascus VA / Laurel Bloomery — verify closures. Pack out waste.

days.planB: Paid campground or motel Bristol if weather or fatigue wins.
feesAndPasses: Blue Ridge Parkway (NPS): no entrance fee for the parkway itself; standard NPS rules apply (speed, wildlife).
Optional stops (e.g. Mabry Mill): parking may fill — no separate park ‘ticket’ beyond your time.

days.title: Bristol → Great Smoky Mountains → Tail of the Dragon → Kingston (TN)
days.routeLine: Bristol → Smokies gateway (Gatlinburg/Newfound Gap optional scenic) → US-129 Tail of the Dragon (Deals Gap) → Kingston TN (Loudon County). US-129 is enforcement-heavy — ride your own pace; crossing centerline is citation bait.
days.fuelNotes: Fuel before Dragon (Deals Gap store) or Knoxville corridor — don’t start Dragon at reserve.
days.foodNotes: Deals Gap / Tellico has limited options — eat before late push.
days.weatherNotes: Mountain showers; Dragon surface can be slick from leaves or dew.
keyNotes: GSMNP: peak season may require parking/entry reservations — check NPS.gov.
Dragon: 318 curves in 11 miles — fatigue kills; pull off often.
days.campingAccommodation: Free dispersed near Tellico / Cherokee NF (verify)
days.terrain: Smokies approaches · US-129 · TN river hills

lodging.heading: Free camping (dispersed)
lodging.name: Cherokee NF — Tellico Plains / Bald River corridor (MVUM)
lodging.notes: Dispersed camping common on NF roads west of Dragon — verify MVUM, fire restrictions, and bear storage. Example pin area: Tellico River corridor — confirm road status.

days.planB: Paid campground Kingston / Loudon if dispersed full.
feesAndPasses: Great Smoky Mountains NP: no entrance fee, but a parking tag is required for any vehicle parked >15 minutes (2026: daily US$5, weekly US$15, annual US$40 — purchase/print via recreation.gov activity pass or in-park; display physical tag per nps.gov/grsm).
Smokies is not on the 2026 US$100 non-resident surcharge list — that applies only to the 11 parks listed on nps.gov/aboutus/nonresident-fees.htm.
Tail of the Dragon (US-129): public highway — no park entrance; obey enforcement and lane rules.

Non-U.S. citizens (2026): America the Beautiful annual pass for non-residents US$250 (digital: recreation.gov/pass) — covers standard entrance fees where accepted and waives the US$100/person non-resident surcharge at the 11 high-visitation parks when you show valid pass + photo ID (see nps.gov/aboutus/nonresident-fees.htm). U.S. resident pass remains US$80.

days.title: Kingston → Parsons (TN)
days.routeLine: Short west Tennessee day — US-70 / I-40 corridor options to Parsons (38363). Visit town, resupply, rest before Arkansas push.
days.weatherNotes: Humid late spring — afternoon storms possible.
keyNotes: Light day — use for laundry, bike check, hydration recovery.
days.campingAccommodation: Free camping — public land / river access (verify)
highlights: Kentucky Lake / Tennessee River region nearby

stops.label: Destination
stops.notes: Verify street parking and local overnight rules if urban edge.

lodging.heading: Free camping (dispersed / primitive)
lodging.name: Natchez Trace State Forest / Kentucky Lake USACE (verify free)
lodging.notes: West TN: search iOverlander for free camps near Parsons / Decaturville — many are lake/river bank or WMA — confirm legal camping, fire rules, and flood line. USACE sometimes offers free primitive areas — read signage.

risks: Ticks/chiggers in tall grass near water
days.planB: County fairground or church parking with permission — ask locally.
feesAndPasses: No major national-park entrance on this short west-TN day. Natchez Trace State Forest / USACE / WMA: follow posted day-use or camping fees if you use a fee area — many dispersed options are free if legally allowed.

days.title: Parsons → Little Rock (AR)

lodging.heading: Stay option
lodging.name: Joe Maglie
lodging.notes: Confirm street number on Harden Dr.

lodgingAlternatives.heading: Stay option
lodgingAlternatives.name: Marci Livingston
lodgingAlternatives.address: 897 Cedar Creek Road, Garland County, AR 71901
lodgingAlternatives.notes: Near Hot Springs National Park area — good rest day option.

feesAndPasses: Hot Springs National Park (optional detour near Garland County): no entrance fee to enter the park (per NPS). Bathhouse row services are separate commercial fees if you use them.
Arkansas state highways: no park pass required for slab transit.

days.title: Little Rock → Fort Worth (TX)

lodging.name: HOME — Fort Worth, TX
lodging.notes: Garage tools, laundry, bike check before western push.

risks: Fast TX highways: cattle guards, expansion joints, and bridge transitions — ease up and square the bike before hitting them leaned over.
feesAndPasses: Texas: possible toll roads on some I-30/I-20 routings — TxTag / toll-by-plate; budget if your GPS uses toll lanes.
No NPS entrance on this home-stop day as written.

days.title: Fort Worth → Amarillo (TX)

lodging.heading: Stay option
lodging.name: Kelly Snyder
lodging.notes: Historic 66 — iconic midpoint vibe.

lodgingAlternatives.heading: Stay option
lodgingAlternatives.name: Matt Moore
lodgingAlternatives.notes: Panhandle alternative north of Amarillo corridor.

risks: Grooved pavement and metal bridge decks at highway speeds — reduce lean and avoid hard braking on seams.
feesAndPasses: Texas plains / Route 66 stops: roadside attractions may charge their own small admission — optional.
No federal park entrance fee for the driving day as written.

days.title: Amarillo → Albuquerque (NM)

stops.label: Route 66 reference
stops.notes: Landmark from your notes — verify pin.

lodging.heading: Camping / motel options
lodging.name: Motel (I-40 frontage)
lodging.address: 6030 W Interstate 40 Frontage Rd, Amarillo, TX area (~$50 CAD — verify)
lodging.notes: ~$50 CAD in your notes; verify property name, side of road, and book ahead.

days.planB: Motel in Amarillo or Albuquerque if weather turns.
feesAndPasses: iOverlander / private camp pins (~US$10 tenting in your notes): pay the host/landowner; not an NPS fee.
I-40 / Amarillo: no NPS fee for driving; motel/camp is commercial pricing.

days.title: Albuquerque → Million Dollar Highway (Silverton, CO)
days.terrain: Climb into San Juan Mountains — snow/ice risk off-season
highlights: Silverton area; Million Dollar Hwy when conditions allow

lodging.heading: Camping
lodging.name: iOverlander (FREE)
lodging.notes: GPS 37.82022, -107.71362 — confirm MVUM / fire rules. Additional free dispersed camping along the route is possible — verify land status.

risks: Mountain chip seal and tar patches — traction drops fast when wet; treat painted lines and metal grates as low-grip.
days.planB: Paid campground or Silverton lodging if dispersed full or weather bad.
feesAndPasses: San Juan NF / Silverton area: USFS — no NPS entrance; dispersed camping usually free where allowed — verify MVUM. Developed USFS or county sites may charge.
Colorado state highways: no park pass for through-riding.

days.title: Silverton → Mexican Hat (UT)

lodging.heading: Camping (~$22 + tax)
lodging.name: Mexican Hat Rocks
lodging.notes: Reserve or confirm same-day availability in season.

feesAndPasses: Mexican Hat Rocks campground (private): ~US$22+ tax in your plan — reserve/confirm on their site (seasonal).
Navajo Nation / Monument Valley (if you enter tribal park): separate tribal permit/fees — not included in America the Beautiful.

days.title: Mexican Hat → Page → Bryce Canyon (~500 km)
days.routeLine: US-163 toward Page → US-89 toward Kanab/Bryce corridor — pin exact path and breaks in your GPS (long mileage day).
days.fuelNotes: Do not pass fuel under half tank; top off leaving Page or Kanab before climbing toward Bryce.
days.foodNotes: Page has services; carry water — long hot segments possible.
days.weatherNotes: Desert heat + isolated afternoon storms — start early; watch crosswinds on open mesas.
keyNotes: Antelope Canyon: guided tours sell out — book ahead.
Horseshoe Bend: paid parking, short hike, brutal midday sun.
End goal: confirm dispersed pin is still legal same week.
days.campingAccommodation: iOverlander pins in this file (options A/B) — verify road grade for your bike and fire restrictions.

stops.label: Horseshoe Bend
stops.notes: Parking fee / short hike — heat and crowds.

stops.label: Antelope Canyon
stops.notes: Book Navajo-guided tour slots in advance.

lodging.heading: iOverlander camping
lodging.name: Dispersed near Bryce (option A)
lodging.notes: GPS 37.65553, -112.17089 — verify fees and road conditions.

lodgingAlternatives.heading: iOverlander camping
lodgingAlternatives.name: Dispersed near Bryce (option B — scenic / fire pit)

days.planB: Shorten: skip one sight or book paid campground inside Bryce area.
feesAndPasses: Bryce Canyon NP (NPS): motorcycle 7-day entrance US$30 (cashless at park — nps.gov/brca); private vehicle US$35. Bryce is on the 2026 non-resident surcharge list — without an America the Beautiful pass, ages 16+ non-U.S. residents pay an additional US$100/person at entry (with valid non-resident annual pass, entrance + surcharge covered for passholder per NPS rules — verify ID).
Horseshoe Bend overlook (City of Page): parking fee ~US$5 motorcycle / ~US$10 private vehicle (Page, AZ — not covered by federal passes; verify current rate at visitpage.com / city site).
Antelope Canyon: Navajo Nation guided tours — commercial pricing (often US$50–100+); book slots in advance; separate from NPS.
Glen Canyon NRA (Lake Powell area): if you use NRA fee areas, Glen Canyon has its own fee schedule — America the Beautiful applies to NPS-managed standard entrance (see nps.gov/glca).

Non-U.S. citizens (2026): America the Beautiful annual pass for non-residents US$250 (digital: recreation.gov/pass) — covers standard entrance fees where accepted and waives the US$100/person non-resident surcharge at the 11 high-visitation parks when you show valid pass + photo ID (see nps.gov/aboutus/nonresident-fees.htm). U.S. resident pass remains US$80.

days.title: Bryce Canyon → Arches National Park (Moab, UT)
days.terrain: UT-12 / scenic byways — traffic in season

lodging.heading: Stay (Moab)
lodging.name: Robert VandenBroeke
lodging.notes: Confirm full address / RV or bike parking.

lodgingAlternatives.heading: Camping (~$20/night)
lodgingAlternatives.name: Camp area (GPS)
lodgingAlternatives.notes: GPS 38.55729, -109.58466 — verify name, amenities, and booking.

risks: Crowds at Arches — arrive early; lots can temporarily close when full
feesAndPasses: Driving UT-12 toward Moab: if you enter Capitol Reef NP, pay NPS entrance (motorcycle rate on nps.gov/care) or use America the Beautiful — optional detour only.
Canyonlands / Arches not yet visited this day as written — no Arches fee until you enter.

days.title: Moab — Arches & Dead Horse Point (Rest/Exploration Day)
days.routeLine: Zero riding day. Base: Moab, UT. Arches NP is 8 km from town — drive or ride out early before the heat and tourist crowds build.
days.fuelNotes: No fuel needed — day ride within Moab area only.
days.terrain: Paved park roads and short walking trails. No challenging riding.
highlights: Arches National Park — Delicate Arch hike (5 km round trip, start by 7 am)
Dead Horse Point State Park — 600 m canyon rim overlook above the Colorado River
Canyonlands NP Island in the Sky (optional 30-min drive from Dead Horse Point)
Moab town: gear shops, resupply, laundry, bike check

stops.label: Arches NP entrance
stops.notes: Pay NPS entrance (motorcycle or vehicle rate) or use America the Beautiful. As of 2026, timed-entry reservations for Arches were discontinued — check nps.gov/arch for any parking/closure updates before you go.

stops.label: Dead Horse Point SP
stops.place: Dead Horse Point State Park, UT
stops.notes: 600 m above the Colorado River — one of the most photographed overlooks in the USA. Entry fee ~$20.

lodging.heading: Stay (Moab — second night)
lodging.name: Robert VandenBroeke
lodging.notes: Second consecutive night — confirm availability with host for 2-night stay.

lodgingAlternatives.heading: Camping (~$20/night)
lodgingAlternatives.name: Camp area (GPS)
lodgingAlternatives.notes: GPS 38.55729, -109.58466 — verify name, amenities, and booking.

lodgingAlternatives.heading: Motel option
lodgingAlternatives.name: Any Moab motel (book ahead)
lodgingAlternatives.notes: Moab fills fast in peak season — if host can only do 1 night, book motel for this night.

risks: Moab summer heat — Arches by 7 am, off trail by 11 am
Arches parking — popular trailheads fill early; carry park map offline
Dehydration risk on Delicate Arch hike — carry 2 L minimum
days.weatherNotes: June temperatures in Moab regularly exceed 38 °C (100 °F) by midday. All outdoor activity should be done before 10–11 am or after 5 pm.
days.planB: If Arches is too crowded or lots are closed, Dead Horse Point + Canyonlands Island in the Sky is an equally spectacular full day.
feesAndPasses: Arches NP: motorcycle 7-day entrance US$25; private vehicle US$30 (nps.gov/arch, credit/debit at entrance). America the Beautiful covers standard entrance. 2026: timed-entry reservation for Arches was discontinued — verify current status on nps.gov/arch before travel (parking may still temporarily limit entry when lots are full).
Fiery Furnace hikes: separate permit/ranger fees if you do those activities (nps.gov/arch).
Dead Horse Point State Park (Utah state): separate from NPS — motorcycle day-use ~US$10, private vehicle ~US$20 (stateparks.utah.gov — verify 2026). Not covered by federal annual pass.
Canyonlands Island in the Sky (optional): NPS entrance fee if you enter — motorcycle rate on nps.gov/cany.

Non-U.S. citizens (2026): America the Beautiful annual pass for non-residents US$250 (digital: recreation.gov/pass) — covers standard entrance fees where accepted and waives the US$100/person non-resident surcharge at the 11 high-visitation parks when you show valid pass + photo ID (see nps.gov/aboutus/nonresident-fees.htm). U.S. resident pass remains US$80.

days.title: Moab → St. George (UT)
highlights: Zion outer approaches possible if time

stops.label: Free camping ideas
stops.place: St. George area — Treks With Beks
stops.notes: Roundup of free camps near St. George.

lodging.heading: BLM / dispersed
lodging.name: Sheep Bridge Park (BLM)
lodging.notes: Confirm road suitability for your bike loadout.

days.planB: Paid RV park or hotel in St. George.
feesAndPasses: BLM dispersed (Sheep Bridge / Cove Wash): typically no entrance fee; follow BLM posted rules.

Zion NP (if you enter the park): Zion is on the 2026 non-resident surcharge list — motorcycle entrance fee + possible US$100/person without annual pass; check nps.gov/zion + tunnel restrictions for large vehicles.

days.title: St. George → Barstow (CA)

lodging.name: Montana Shaffer
lodging.notes: Apple Valley — near Barstow / Victorville; confirm ETA for late Mojave arrivals.

feesAndPasses: I-15: possible toll lanes in some segments — account for toll-by-plate.
No park entrance fee for Mojave transit to Apple Valley as written.

days.title: Barstow → Hollywood → Leo Carrillo (Day Stop) → Big Sur / Prewitt Ridge
days.routeLine: Barstow → I-15 S → CA-14 → LA basin → PCH (Hwy 1) north through Malibu → Leo Carrillo State Park (2-3 hr day stop) → PCH north through Big Sur → Prewitt Ridge Campground. ~590 km total — longest riding day of the California leg. Start by 6:30 am to clear LA traffic.
days.fuelNotes: Fuel in Barstow before departure. Next reliable fuel: Cambria or San Simeon (before Big Sur no-services stretch). Fill there.
days.terrain: CA freeways to coast — read current California DMV / CHP lane-splitting guidance; only allowed in specific situations. PCH curves, tar snakes, patch pavement, damp fog — reduced grip even when dry. Prewitt Ridge final climb: rough dirt road.
highlights: First sight of the Pacific Ocean after 17 days inland — emotional milestone for video
Leo Carrillo State Park (Malibu) — 2-3 hr stop: swim, beach content, food
PCH north through Ventura, Santa Barbara, and San Luis Obispo county
Bixby Creek Bridge — iconic Big Sur arch bridge photo stop
McWay Falls / Julia Pfeiffer Burns SP — short walk, waterfall into the ocean
Prewitt Ridge — BLM dispersed camp with Pacific view

stops.label: Day stop — Leo Carrillo
stops.place: Leo Carrillo State Park, Malibu, CA
stops.notes: 2-3 hour stop. Beach, swim, content creation — this is your Pacific arrival moment. No overnight here.

stops.label: Food / fuel resupply
stops.notes: Last reliable fuel before Big Sur. Fill tank and carry extra food — limited services on Hwy 1 north.

stops.label: Photo stop — Bixby Creek Bridge
stops.place: Bixby Creek Bridge, Big Sur, CA
stops.notes: Pull over on north side for the classic arch-over-coast shot.

lodging.name: Prewitt Ridge Campground
lodging.notes: Confirm road conditions for loaded DR650; carry 4 L water. Final climb is rough BLM dirt. Arrive before dark.

risks: LA traffic — depart Barstow by 6:30 am to beat morning rush
PCH weekend parking pressure at Malibu
Big Sur: Coastal Hwy 1 tar snakes, patch pavement, damp fog — reduced grip in corners
Prewitt Ridge: cliff exposure on approach road, private gate policies — verify access
Long day (~590 km) — monitor fatigue; Cambria is a bail-out overnight if needed
days.planB: If fatigued by Cambria: stay at state park camp (Hwy 1 corridor) and ride Prewitt Ridge the next morning before continuing north.
feesAndPasses: Leo Carrillo State Park (CA): vehicle day-use fee required (California State Parks — 2026 pricing on parks.ca.gov; online day-use reservation recommended weekends/holidays).
PCH / Big Sur: Julia Pfeiffer Burns SP and other CA state parks charge day-use or parking fees — check parks.ca.gov for each stop.
Prewitt Ridge BLM: usually no fee; confirm BLM Los Padres info for your exact spur.

days.title: Prewitt Ridge → Point Arena Lighthouse

lodging.heading: Camping (verify fees)
lodging.name: Dispersed / listed GPS
lodging.notes: GPS 35.87322, -121.41850 — confirm land status and fees before paying/staying.

risks: Regional wildfire smoke can drop visibility and AQI with little warning — check smoke/air maps along the coast that morning.
feesAndPasses: CA Hwy 1 / dispersed pin: confirm land manager (private / county / state) and any day-use or camping fee before paying or camping.

days.title: Point Arena → Redwood National Park

lodging.heading: Camping (paid)
lodging.name: Redwood area camp (GPS)
lodging.notes: GPS 38.98170, -123.70057 — verify campground name and reservation rules.

risks: NorCal / redwoods corridor: summer wildfire smoke and poor AQI — have a shorter-day or inland detour mindset if maps go red.
feesAndPasses: Redwood National and State Parks: NPS units — no entrance fee for the federal Redwood areas (nps.gov/redw). Adjacent California state park units may charge day-use — pay if you enter those units.

days.title: Redwood → Crater Lake (Westfir, OR)
days.terrain: OR coastal range / I-5 or 199 options
highlights: Crater Lake possible as detour if open and time allows

lodging.name: Deb & Dave B
lodging.notes: Confirm exact address / directions into town.

risks: Wildfire smoke and AQI — not only “late summer”; June can see regional haze from distant fires. Check AQI and visibility each morning across OR / PNW / Rockies legs.
Heavy smoke: favor shorter riding, more breaks, and indoor air when AQI is unhealthy — especially at altitude.
feesAndPasses: Crater Lake NP (optional detour): NPS entrance applies if you enter — motorcycle rate on nps.gov/crla; America the Beautiful accepted. Not on the 11-park US$100 surcharge list as published Jan 2026 (verify if policy updates).

days.title: Westfir → Goldendale / Mosier

lodging.heading: Stay option
lodging.name: Greg Wagner
lodging.notes: WA side of gorge region.

lodgingAlternatives.heading: Stay option
lodgingAlternatives.name: Darin
lodgingAlternatives.notes: OR side — good for gorge riding flow.

feesAndPasses: Columbia River Gorge NSA / OR & WA state parks: some trailheads require Northwest Forest Pass or state day-use — check fs.usda.gov and Oregon/Washington park sites for the exact stop.

days.title: Mosier → Seattle (WA)
highlights: Three host segments around Seattle metro

lodging.heading: Pre-Seattle
lodging.name: Bob Combs
lodging.notes: Cascade foothills — good last resupply before metro. Treat tonight as recovery / city arrival: light riding tomorrow if you stay in the metro; get food, sleep, and optional Seattle content without pushing hard.

lodgingAlternatives.heading: Post-Seattle (east side)
lodgingAlternatives.name: Jennifer Long & Brian Forsyth
lodgingAlternatives.notes: If routing north/east out of Seattle next day.

risks: Splitting stays — pack light for moving between hosts
days.planB: Single hotel hub if multi-host logistics are tight.
feesAndPasses: Seattle metro: no NPS entrance for urban riding; paid parking garages may apply downtown.

days.title: Seattle → US-20 (~400 km)
days.terrain: North Cascades Highway (if open) vs. I-90 fallback
highlights: North Cascades if seasonal opening aligns

stops.label: TBD
stops.notes: Pick town based on North Cascades opening and weather.

lodging.name: TBD — book after route pinned
lodging.notes: Options: Winthrop, Twisp, Omak, or I-90 corridor motels.

risks: PNW / interior BC smoke can funnel into mountain valleys — if AQI spikes, SR-20 vs I-90 isn’t just a snow decision; air quality matters for long climb days.
days.planB: I-90 direct to eastern WA if SR-20 closed.
feesAndPasses: North Cascades National Park complex: no entrance fee to drive WA SR-20 (nps.gov/noca).
If you use a fee trailhead or Sno-Park in season, Washington Sno-Park permit may apply in winter — June usually not applicable.

days.title: US-20 → Spokane

lodging.heading: Stay option
lodging.name: David & Pita Ettenberg
lodging.notes: Confirm address and garage access.

lodgingAlternatives.heading: Stay option
lodgingAlternatives.name: Rathdrum, ID
lodgingAlternatives.notes: ID side alternative if pacing or lodging fits better.

feesAndPasses: Eastern WA / Spokane: no NPS fee for routing as written.

days.title: Spokane → Kooskia → Lolo Pass
highlights: Clearwater / Lochsa River approaches to Lolo

lodging.heading: Stay option
lodging.name: Dustin Patton
lodging.notes: Missoula west side alternative.

days.planB: US-12 services thin — fuel when you can.
feesAndPasses: Lolo Pass / US-12: scenic highway — no national-park entrance fee for the pass transit itself.

days.title: Lolo Pass → Yellowstone
days.terrain: MT high country into Greater Yellowstone
highlights: West Yellowstone or north entrance options depending on route

lodging.name: Deryk Hagey
lodging.notes: Gateway to Yellowstone — confirm park entry timing and parking.

risks: Greater Yellowstone: wildfire smoke and park-wide haze can persist for days — watch NPS alerts and AQI before committing to long high-elevation legs.
feesAndPasses: Yellowstone NP: motorcycle 7-day entrance US$30 (nps.gov/yell). Yellowstone is on the 2026 non-resident surcharge list — without America the Beautiful, ages 16+ non-U.S. residents pay US$100/person in addition to entrance; valid America the Beautiful (including non-resident US$250 pass) covers entrance and surcharge for passholder presentation with ID per NPS.
Keep receipt — 7-day Yellowstone entrance valid for re-entry.

Non-U.S. citizens (2026): America the Beautiful annual pass for non-residents US$250 (digital: recreation.gov/pass) — covers standard entrance fees where accepted and waives the US$100/person non-resident surcharge at the 11 high-visitation parks when you show valid pass + photo ID (see nps.gov/aboutus/nonresident-fees.htm). U.S. resident pass remains US$80.

days.title: Yellowstone National Park — full day
highlights: Geysers, canyon, Lamar or Hayden if routing allows

stops.label: Plan
stops.place: Yellowstone loop — prioritize north or south loop
stops.notes: Check NPS.gov for road closures and construction same season.

lodging.name: Same as Day 26 or move closer to NE exit
lodging.notes: Book inside-park or Gardiner/Cooke if pushing toward Beartooth next day.

risks: Smoke from regional fires can cut views and air quality inside the park — adjust expectations and rest if you feel tight-chested at altitude.
feesAndPasses: Yellowstone NP: same 7-day entrance window if within validity from purchase — carry pass/receipt.

Non-U.S. citizens (2026): America the Beautiful annual pass for non-residents US$250 (digital: recreation.gov/pass) — covers standard entrance fees where accepted and waives the US$100/person non-resident surcharge at the 11 high-visitation parks when you show valid pass + photo ID (see nps.gov/aboutus/nonresident-fees.htm). U.S. resident pass remains US$80.

days.title: Yellowstone → Beartooth Highway → Powell, WY
days.routeLine: Morning: ride Yellowstone’s Grand Loop toward the Northeast Entrance (from wherever you stayed — e.g. Old Faithful area). At Cooke City, MT, pick up US-212 (Beartooth Highway) over the pass to Red Lodge — not WY-310 (Chief Joseph toward Cody) unless 212 is closed. Continue into the Bighorn Basin to Powell, WY for the night. The app’s Maps distance (~316 km) is the Cooke City → Red Lodge → Powell leg only; add your in-park miles separately.
days.fuelNotes: Fill before leaving the park interior; top up in Cooke City or Red Lodge; Powell has services before overnight.
days.terrain: US-212 high alpine — snow/ice possible early season; do not confuse with lower WY-310 corridor
highlights: Beartooth Pass — one of the great US motorcycle roads

lodging.heading: Stay option
lodging.name: Daryn Engesser
lodging.notes: Powell matches the ~316 km Beartooth+Bighorn corridor (Cooke City → Red Lodge → Powell). Confirm address and bike parking.

lodgingAlternatives.heading: Stay option
lodgingAlternatives.name: Jerry Bellmyer
lodgingAlternatives.notes: South of Powell in the Bighorn Basin — short ride from Powell if you prefer this host.

risks: Ice / closure early season — verify pass status
Google Maps often prefers WY-310 / Cody corridor from the park — manually select US-212 via Cooke City and Red Lodge for the Beartooth
days.planB: If US-212 is closed, use NPS/MDT detour guidance (may include lower basin roads — not the same ride as the Beartooth).
feesAndPasses: Yellowstone NP: still inside 7-day window if continuous from prior entries — confirm dates on your pass stub.
Beartooth Highway (US-212): scenic corridor — no separate ‘Beartooth pass’ beyond any NPS Yellowstone re-entry if you dip back in.

days.title: Powell → Gillette (WY)
days.routeLine: Bighorn Basin crossing after the night in Powell — slab and wind typical toward Gillette (Powder River Basin).

lodging.name: Sherry Smith
lodging.notes: Confirm street address.

feesAndPasses: Wyoming plains: no NPS fee for routing as written.

days.title: Gillette → Needles Highway → border camping
days.terrain: Black Hills — Needles / Iron Mountain Road traffic
highlights: Custer State Park / Needles Highway — fees and traffic

lodging.heading: Free camping
lodging.name: TBD — border / Black Hills dispersed
lodging.notes: Pick legal dispersed or USFS after confirming MVUM; next leg starts Oacoma SD area.

days.planB: Paid campground in Black Hills NF or Custer area.
feesAndPasses: Custer State Park (SD): park entrance license required for Wildlife Loop etc. — 2026: 7-day temporary license ~US$25/private vehicle or ~US$20/motorcycle (verify gfp.sd.gov/pel). Non-stop through-travel on designated US-16A through-route may be exempt — read posted rules.
Black Hills National Forest: generally no entrance fee; amenity sites may charge.
Mount Rushmore NM: parking is a private concession fee (not covered by America the Beautiful for the parking structure) — budget separately if you visit.

days.title: Oacoma → Rochester (MN) (~600 km)
days.terrain: Great Plains long haul — services spacing
highlights: Missouri River crossing near Oacoma corridor

stops.label: Fuel strategy
stops.notes: Long day — top off whenever under half tank.

lodging.heading: Stay option
lodging.name: Laurie Calhoon
lodging.notes: Near Rochester.

lodgingAlternatives.heading: Stay option
lodgingAlternatives.name: Carol B
lodgingAlternatives.notes: South of Rochester on I-35 corridor.

risks: Great Plains: persistent wind grooves and open-deck bridges — relax grip, don’t fight the bars, and plan hand breaks.
days.planB: Split into two shorter days if weather or energy is poor.
feesAndPasses: I-90 Great Plains: possible toll segments (e.g. Illinois/Iowa depending on route) — verify GPS toll settings.

days.title: Rochester → Oconomowoc (WI)

lodging.heading: Stay option
lodging.name: Jane Thompson
lodging.notes: Confirm address.

feesAndPasses: Upper Midwest freeways: tolls possible (e.g. Illinois Tollway if routed) — I-Pass or pay-by-plate.

days.title: Oconomowoc → Benton Harbor (Day Stop, John Cody Smith) → London, ON
days.routeLine: Oconomowoc, WI → I-94 E → Benton Harbor, MI (visit John Cody Smith, 2 hr lunch stop) → US-12 or I-94 E → Port Huron, MI → Blue Water Bridge → Sarnia, ON → Hwy 402 E → London, ON. ~530 km total. Border crossing same day (late June) — check CBSA wait times; summer weekday traffic on I-94 can be heavy.
days.fuelNotes: Fuel in Oconomowoc before departure. Fill again at Port Huron before the border — Canadian fuel is priced in litres and typically higher. Cross with a full tank.
days.terrain: I-94 corridor — flat Midwest freeway. Blue Water Bridge (Port Huron–Sarnia): active CBSA crossing. Highway 402 to London: Ontario freeway, smooth.
highlights: John Cody Smith visit (Benton Harbor area) — 2 hr lunch + content stop
Lake Michigan shoreline approach on I-94
Blue Water Bridge at Port Huron/Sarnia — re-entering Canada
Canadian border crossing: note CBSA declaration requirements (duty-free limits after 48 hrs)
London, ON overnight — final Canadian city before Toronto

stops.label: Day stop — John Cody Smith
stops.place: Coloma, MI (near Benton Harbor / St. Joseph)
stops.notes: 2 hr lunch and content stop. Confirm arrival window with John. No overnight — continuing to London, ON same day.

stops.label: Border crossing
stops.place: Blue Water Bridge, Port Huron, MI → Sarnia, ON
stops.notes: Have passport/NEXUS ready for the crossing. Declare all items accurately to CBSA.

lodging.name: TBD — book London, ON
lodging.notes: Any central London motel — book in advance. Last night before home leg tomorrow.

risks: Long day (~530 km) — US-side traffic heavy mid-day; plan Benton Harbor stop early (11 am–1 pm) and border crossing with buffer
Blue Water Bridge border wait — check CBSA wait times that morning; have all documents ready
Michigan I-94 summer traffic — allow extra time around Chicago-to-Detroit corridor
Canadian CBSA: strict about alcohol, firearms, and food items — declare everything
days.weatherNotes: Late June in the Great Lakes region: typically warm (25–32 °C), possible afternoon thunderstorms. Check radar before departure.
days.planB: If John Cody Smith visit runs long or traffic is severe, skip London and stay in Sarnia/Windsor area instead.
feesAndPasses: U.S. side: no national park entrance for I-94 day as written.
Blue Water Bridge (Port Huron → Sarnia): toll per bridge authority — check current motorcycle/car rate and payment method.
Canada (CBSA): no park fee; declare goods per CBSA rules; possible duties on purchases.

days.title: Return home (London, ON → Toronto)

lodging.name: HOME — Toronto, ON
lodging.notes: Final leg from London — debrief, service bike, archive receipts.

feesAndPasses: Ontario 401: 407 ETR if used is all-electronic toll to plate — avoid or register.
Home — no park fee.

documents: E111 or travel medical / evacuation (confirm coverage in US)
bike: Tool kit, tire repair, pump/CO2, spare tubes if applicable
Chain lube, zip ties, electrical tape, spare fuses
Phone mount + USB power, offline maps downloaded
ridingGear: Moisture-wicking base layers for humid Southeast / Mississippi valley legs (easier than cooking in cotton under armor)
camp: Tent / sleep system for high-altitude cold nights
Water capacity for UT/AZ/CA desert legs

emergency.notes: ICE: add contacts and any allergies. Yellowstone: nearest care may be far — carry first aid. If regional wildfire smoke drives AQI into unhealthy ranges, shorten the riding day and seek clean indoor air — especially at altitude or with any respiratory history.

beforeYouGo: Trip window in this file: 2026-05-20 → 2026-06-24. If you move departure, update trip.startDate, trip.endDate, and every days[].date in sync (or regenerate from your spreadsheet).

Maps: set GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY in .env (see .env.example), run npm run build, then serve. On Vercel, add the same variable under Environment Variables — build generates google-maps-config.js. Restrict the key by HTTP referrer (localhost + your Vercel URL). Re-check each leg in Maps after you set exact pins.
Bunk a Biker stays: full host contact cards (phone, text, email, Gmail, draft message) render on each day that uses a host — primary hosts only in bab-hosts.json for this build.
Confirm each host address, gate codes, and bike parking before arrival day.
Arches NP: verify current entry and parking rules on nps.gov/arch for your dates (timed-entry reservations were discontinued as of 2026 research — lots can still fill).
Yellowstone / Beartooth: verify pass opening and NPS alerts.

Every other national park or monument you actually enter on this loop (e.g. Zion tunnel / vehicle size rules, Smokies parking, timed shuttles, Navajo Nation tour permits): open NPS.gov or the managing tribe/agency site for **your** entry dates — rules and reservations change by season.

Surface hazards common on US Interstates and plains: grooved pavement, tar snakes, steel bridge decks, expansion joints, and cattle guards — scan ahead, reduce lean, and avoid hard throttle or braking on transitions.
Download offline maps for the full loop and share location with one trusted contact.
